channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: DOJ calls on Supreme Court to deny Trump's request to delay TikTok ban
item.description: The Justice Department urged the Supreme Court on Friday to deny President-elect Trump’s request to postpone their ruling on a TikTok ban set to be implemented the day before his inauguration.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Net neutrality never stood a chance
item.description: A federal appeals court sealed the fate of the Biden administration’s efforts to regulate internet providers on Thursday, striking down the Federal Communications Commission’s 2024 net neutrality order once and for all.
item.dc:creator: Issie Lapowsky, Fast Company

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took police more than a week to publicly identify Debrina Kawam, 57, as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train last month. But on the internet, it took just hours for a false name to begin spreading.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Chinese cyber firm linked to botnet attack sanctioned in US
item.description: The federal government hit Chinese cyber group Integrity Technology Group with new sanctions on Friday, citing alleged computer intrusion incidents against those in the U.S. At the direction of the China, hackers associated with the Integrity Technology Group allegedly targeted multiple U.S.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Why Apple TV+ is offering a free weekend of binge-watching
item.description: Apple TV+ is hoping people will make a dent in the Strategic Popcorn Reserve by bingeing its streaming TV and movies for free this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Mark Kennedy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: I love the look of these dynamic wallpapers that were supposedly for Windows 11 – but the idea has been scrapped
item.description: Windows 11’s rumored dynamic wallpapers look seriously smart – but they’ll never brighten your desktop sadly.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: A double dose of nasty winter is about to smack much of the US with snow, ice and biting cold
item.description: Meteorologists forecast a strong snow and ice storm followed by brutally cold conditions will soon smack the eastern two-thirds of the United States.
item.dc:creator: By Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: US PC prices could soar if Trump tariff threats come true
item.description: Proposed import tariffs for the US could see PC costs rise as much as 46%, with smartphones also becoming more expensive.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Hisense's new 4K laser TV delivers up to 150 inches of picture, and 6.2.2 channels of Dolby Atmos sound
item.description: Hisense has revealed its new laser TV ahead of CES 2025, and it's going big on both projection size and sound quality.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The cloud architecture renaissance of 2025
item.description: The perfect storm is coming that will force enterprises to rethink their cloud strategy. Cloud architecture will take center stage during 2025. This isn’t just another hype cycle. First, we need to talk about the elephant in the room: generative AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 25 experts predict how AI will change business and life in 2025
item.description: The so-called AI boom has been going on for more than two years now, and 2024 saw a real acceleration in both the development and the application of the technology.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: California begins 2025 with solid start to winter snowpack, but more storms are needed
item.description: California is beginning 2025 with a solid start to the winter snowpack, but more storms are needed to keep the state’s water supplies on track.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tesla Cybertruck joins short list of EVs eligible for $7,500 tax credit
item.description: The number of EVs and plug-in hybrids currently qualified for a credit is 18 models, down from 22 last year.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Microsoft raises bar with water-saving data center design
item.description: Microsoft’s new data center designs include a cooling system that it claims will consume zero water while at the same time offering liquid cooling of the hardware. The designs leverage a chip-level liquid cooling solution that can provide precise temperature control without water evaporation.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Musk defends demonetizing critics' content on X
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk defended his move to demonetize his critics' content on his social platform X and maintained the decision did not violate free speech. "The first amendment is protection for 'free speech', not 'paid speech' ffs," Musk wrote Wednesday on X.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: IA generativa en 2025: de la experimentación a la práctica real
item.description: Parece llegar a su fin el tiempo de que las empresas jueguen con la IA generativa: las organizaciones comienzan una nueva etapa en la que se reduce la experimentación para pivotar hacia logros de valor empresarial, con un enfoque en menos casos de uso pero más específicos.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Ser CIO en 2025: estos serán los principales desafíos tecnológicos
item.description: Anticipar cuáles serán las tendencias en tecnología para el próximo año tiene un poco de bola de cristal: su propio carácter disruptivo hace que en ocasiones entre en juego una herramienta que hasta entonces no había destacado.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Managing Social Media Boundaries at Work: Tips from the Posting Playbook
item.description: Learn how to navigate social media boundaries with your boss and coworkers, and discover tips for handling online flirting in the workplace.
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: Antioch brackish desalination plant set to begin operations
item.description: Antioch's brackish desalination plant will kickstart operations in the first quarter of 2025. It is expected to treat and convert 6 million gallons a day of brackish water.
item.dc:creator: Hema Sivanandam, The Mercury News

item.title: How to use Gemini AI to make templates in Google Sheets
item.description: Need ideas or motivation to help you build a spreadsheet in Google Sheets? You can browse through the templates that are included in this office app and select one to customize. But a more intriguing option is to use the tool in Sheets called Help Me Organize.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Venezuela's top court issues a $10 million fine for TikTok over allegedly deadly video challenges
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuela’s Supreme Court on Monday issued a $10 million fine against TikTok for “not implementing measures” to prevent viral video challenges that have allegedly led to the deaths of three Venezuelan children recently.
item.dc:creator: JUAN PABLO ARRAEZ and MANUEL RUEDA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hackers have posted some RIBridges data on the dark web, McKee says
item.description: The state disclosed earlier this month that cybercriminals had gained access to the state's IT system for social services.
item.dc:creator: Ted Nesi, NewsNation

item.title: Verizon, Nvidia team up for enterprise AI networking
item.description: Verizon and Nvidia have partnered to build AI services for enterprises that run workloads over Verizon’s 5G private network. The new offering, 5G Private Network with Enterprise AI, will run a range of AI applications and workloads over Verizon’s private 5G network with Mobile Edge Compute (MEC).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Hands-on with Adobe’s Acrobat AI Assistant: AI for your PDFs
item.description: We may be on the brink of 2025, but PDFs are still unavoidable in the professional world. No matter what industry you work in, you’re bound to whittle away precious moments wading through reports, white papers, and other dense documents in that clunky-feeling form.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
